items.title: What to Expect From Wall Street Banks Earnings

items.title: PE Fund Harith Gets Antitrust Approval to Acquire FlySafair
items.description: South Africa’s antitrust regulator approved private equity firm Harith General Partners’ proposal to acquire budget carrier FlySafair.

items.title: UK’s Miliband Seen as Least Appealing Chancellor: Markets Pulse
items.description: Andy Burnham has yet to announce his choice for UK Chancellor of the Exchequer, but among the presumptive next prime minister’s leading candidates, Energy Secretary Ed Miliband is seen as the least ma

items.title: China’s Green-Tech Exports Surge on Energy Transition Demand
items.description: China’s exports of green-tech products rose by more than a third in the first half of the year, driven by the accelerating global energy transition.

items.title: Indonesia Says S&P Affirmation to Help Stabilize Bond Market
items.description: Indonesia expects S&P Global Ratings’ vote of confidence in its sovereign credit profile to help stabilize the domestic bond market after a selloff in June, according to a top finance ministry officia

items.title: China Expels Former Politburo Member Over Graft and Sex Charges
items.description: China expelled former Politburo member Ma Xingrui from the ruling Communist Party, accusing him of a wide range of offenses including corruption, abuse of power and trading political favors for sex.

items.title: South Korean Won Climbs to Two-Month High on Hynix ADR Inflows
items.description: The South Korean won advanced to its strongest level since May on expectations that dollar inflows from SK Hynix Inc’s American depositary receipts will enter the local currency market.

items.title: Palm Oil Advances as Crude Rally Boosts Outlook for Biofuels
items.description: Palm oil climbed for a second day as a surge in crude prices fueled by renewed US-Iran hostilities brightened demand prospects for biofuels.

items.title: South Korea Plans FX Rule Easing to Expand Won’s Global Use

items.title: Australia Sees No Mass Layoffs From AI Just Yet

items.title: US, Iran Check the Oil Price Before Pushing Limits
items.description: Trump’s 20% levy is the latest example of seeing what they can get away with.

items.title: Japan Bond Sale Draws High Demand After Katayama’s GPIF Remarks
items.description: Japan’s 20-year government bond auction Tuesday attracted strong investor demand after remarks by Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama boosted sentiment toward sovereign debt.

items.title: China Trade Numbers Top All Forecasts in June 2026
items.description: Here's a quick look at the numbers as China's exports surge in June while imports rise at the fastest clip in five years.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Iran Sneaking Out Tankers Via Hormuz as Trump Amps Up Threats
items.description: Iran has been sneaking oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz over the past few days, even before the US said it would reimpose a blockade of Iranian ports as hostilities escalated.

items.title: Yuan Still Cheap Versus Euro Even After Gain, Deutsche Bank Says
items.description: China’s yuan is still undervalued versus the euro despite its recent gains, and that’s contributing to a wider European Union trade deficit, according to Deutsche Bank AG.
